title: Lemon Risotto
excerpt: Ingredients 2 litres chicken or vegetable stock 90 ml butter 30 ml olive oil 125 ml finely chopped onion 500 ml Arborio or Risotto rice 90 ml dry white wine 250 ml grated Parmesan cheese 65 ml finely chopped parsley 45 ml fresh lemon juice 30 ml finely grated lemon zest Method Heat the stock to boiling point. Lower the heat …
html:
Ingredients

  • 2 litres chicken or vegetable stock
  • 90 ml butter
  • 30 ml olive oil
  • 125 ml finely chopped onion
  • 500 ml Arborio or Risotto rice
  • 90 ml dry white wine
  • 250 ml grated Parmesan cheese
  • 65 ml finely chopped parsley
  • 45 ml fresh lemon juice
  • 30 ml finely grated lemon zest

Method

Heat the stock to boiling point. Lower the heat and keep warm.

In a large pot, melt half the butter with the oil and fry the onion until soft and glossy.

Add the rice to the onion and stir for a minute or until glossy. Add the wine and stir until absorbed.

Now add 375 ml (about two soup spoons) of the stock to the rice and let it simmer while stirring constantly, until the rice has absorbed most of the liquid. Repeat this with 125 ml of the stock at a time while continuing to stir, until the rice has absorbed the liquid. Keep up the process (for about half an hour) until the rice is soft but still has a slight 'bite' in the centre of the grain. If it isn't runny enough, add water and stir thoroughly to make the mixture creamy.

Now stir in the rest of the butter, cheese, parsley, lemon juice and zest. Season to taste and serve immediately.

Makes 4 – 6 servings depending on whether it's the main course or a side dish.

Worth knowing: A true, proper risotto should be slightly runny – almost saucy – and should behave like porridge. If you spoon it into a soup plate and shake the plate from side to side, the rice should spread flat, like porridge. It shouldn't stay in a heap.

Easy!
